senses.definition: a type of sport in which a person climbs rock surfaces that are very steep

examples.x: Rock climbing requires strength and focus.
She enjoys indoor rock climbing at the local gym.
Rock climbing is a popular outdoor adventure sport.
He practiced rock climbing on a steep cliff.
The group joined a rock climbing class for beginners.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of climbing frozen waterfalls, ice-covered rock faces, or glaciers using specialized equipment like ice axes and crampons

examples.x: Ice climbing requires strength and technique to navigate frozen surfaces.
He trained for months before attempting ice climbing in the Alps.
Ice climbing is a thrilling but challenging outdoor activity.
She went ice climbing with a group of experienced mountaineers.
The gear for ice climbing includes crampons, ropes, and an ice axe.

senses.definition: a style of climbing where climbers place their own protective gear as they climb instead of relying on pre-existing bolts

examples.x: Traditional climbing requires careful placement of gear to ensure safety.
They set out early in the morning for a day of traditional climbing in the mountains.
Traditional climbing can be more challenging but also more rewarding.
The group discussed the ethics of traditional climbing versus sport climbing.
She learned traditional climbing from experienced mentors in the climbing community.

senses.definition: a style of rock climbing where climbers ascend without the use of ropes or protective gear

examples.x: Free solo climbing is considered one of the most dangerous forms of climbing.
Safety is paramount in free solo climbing, with no room for error.
Free solo climbing requires immense skill, focus, and confidence.
She attempted her first free solo climbing on a beginner-friendly route.
Experts advise against attempting free solo climbing without proper training and experience.

senses.definition: a form of rock climbing that relies on permanent anchors for protection rather than traditional gear placement

examples.x: She's been training for months to improve her technique in sport climbing.
Sport climbing involves ascending pre-placed bolts and anchors for safety.
Sport climbing competitions test both strength and strategy.
The sport climbing route was challenging, requiring precise footwork and grip.
Sport climbing has gained popularity as both a recreational activity and competitive sport.

senses.definition: a form of rock climbing where climbers ascend cliffs above water without the use of ropes, relying on the water below for protection in case of a fall

examples.x: Deep-water soloing requires strong swimming skills in case of a fall.
Deep-water soloing offers a thrilling experience for climbers seeking adventure.
Deep-water soloing combines the challenges of climbing with the excitement of water sports.

senses.definition: a sport where athletes compete to climb artificial or natural rock structures within a specified time or without falling

examples.x: Climbers from different countries gather to compete in international competition climbing tournaments.
Competition climbing tests climbers' skills in speed, lead, and bouldering disciplines.
He excels in bouldering, a discipline within competition climbing.
Winning a medal in competition climbing requires both physical prowess and strategic planning.
The rules of competition climbing vary depending on the discipline and competition format.

senses.definition: a style of rock climbing performed on small rock formations or artificial rock walls without the use of ropes or harnesses

examples.x: Bouldering is a popular form of climbing that emphasizes strength and technique.
He spent the afternoon bouldering at the local climbing gym.
The bouldering area in the park attracts climbers of all skill levels.
She's working on improving her grip strength for better bouldering performance.

senses.definition: a style of climbing where the climber ascends a route while attaching the rope to protection points along the way, placing gear for safety

examples.x: Lead climbing requires climbers to clip into protection as they ascend.
He excelled at lead climbing due to his ability to manage rope and gear efficiently.
Lead climbing tests a climber's ability to handle fear and make quick decisions.
Many climbers prefer lead climbing for its sense of adventure and challenge.
Lead climbing competitions showcase athletes' speed and technical skill.

senses.definition: a competitive discipline where climbers ascend a standardized route as quickly as possible

examples.x: She set a new personal record in speed climbing at the local gym.
Speed climbing tests a climber's agility, strength, and endurance.
Speed climbing competitions feature head-to-head races up vertical walls.
He trained rigorously to improve his speed climbing technique.
Speed climbing is one of the disciplines in the Olympic sport of climbing.

senses.definition: a style of rock climbing where climbers use gear to assist their ascent by pulling or standing on equipment rather than relying solely on natural holds

examples.x: Aid climbing involves using specialized equipment to ascend vertical walls.
They practiced aid climbing techniques before attempting the challenging route.
The team used a variety of cams and pitons for aid climbing.
He learned to trust his gear while aid climbing in the mountains.
Proper training is crucial for safe and successful aid climbing expeditions.

senses.definition: the practice of ascending a route using both ice tools and traditional rock climbing techniques

examples.x: Mixed climbing demands proficiency in both ice and rock techniques.
She enjoys the challenge of mixed climbing, navigating varied terrain.
Proper equipment is crucial for safety in mixed climbing expeditions.
Many climbers find mixed climbing exhilarating due to its diverse challenges.
He's training intensively to improve his skills in mixed climbing.

senses.definition: the act of climbing on artificial walls using ropes and harnesses for safety and training

examples.x: Indoor climbing gyms provide a safe environment for beginners to learn the basics.
Climbers use specialized shoes and harnesses for indoor climbing.
Indoor climbing is a popular activity for team building and group outings.
Rainy days are perfect for indoor climbing when outdoor conditions are unfavorable.
Indoor climbing routes are often changed to keep the experience fresh for regular visitors.

senses.definition: a style of climbing that involves ascending a rock face in multiple stages, with each stage requiring a separate rope length

examples.x: Multi-pitch climbing demands teamwork and communication between climbers.
They strategized their route for the multi-pitch climbing adventure.
Multi-pitch climbing often requires intermediate belays for safety.
The view from the summit after multi-pitch climbing was breathtaking.
She enjoyed the variety of challenges that multi-pitch climbing offered.

senses.definition: (climbing) the most challenging or difficult part of a route or climb

examples.x: The crux of the climb required precise footwork and strong technique.
She struggled to find the right hold at the crux of the route.
Many climbers spend extra time strategizing how to overcome the crux.
Reaching the crux of the climb tested his mental and physical endurance.
Climbers often celebrate reaching and conquering the crux of a challenging route.

senses.definition: to successfully complete a climbing route from start to finish without falling or resting on the rope, typically after multiple attempts
(climbing) to successfully complete a route on the first attempt without any prior knowledge or information about the route's challenges

examples.x: He managed to onsight the difficult route, relying solely on his instincts and skill.
The climber's ability to onsight various routes impressed the spectators.
They set out to onsight a series of routes during their climbing trip.
He felt a rush of adrenaline as he approached the crux of the route, aiming to onsight it flawlessly.
Despite the route's tricky sections, he managed to onsight it with grace.

senses.definition: (climbing) to successfully complete a route on the first attempt, typically without prior knowledge or practice

examples.x: He managed to flash the difficult route on his first try.
She was thrilled to flash the challenging boulder problem.
The climber's ability to flash routes impressed the entire group.
She set a personal goal to flash every route in the gym.
He flashed the route effortlessly, surprising even himself.

senses.definition: (climbing) to use the friction of one's shoe against the rock surface to gain traction and support

examples.x: He had to smear on the smooth rock face to find purchase.
The climber relied on precise foot placements to smear effectively.
She struggled to smear on the slick limestone cliff during the rain.
The climber's technique involved delicately smearing on the tiny edges.

senses.definition: a climbing technique that involves using opposing pressure between two surfaces to ascend without relying on handholds

examples.x: Stemming saved him from a fall on the wide crack.
The route required skilled stemming to navigate the featureless wall.
Stemming proved crucial on the steep, smooth face of the rock.

senses.definition: a climbing technique where climbers use the edges of their shoes to stand on small footholds

examples.x: Edging is crucial for maintaining balance on steep, technical climbs.
She relied on precise edging to navigate the tiny footholds on the route.

senses.definition: a grip in climbing where the climber pulls outward with their hand turned thumb-down and palm facing inward

examples.x: She used a gaston to stabilize herself on the overhanging wall.
The route required a strong gaston to progress past the crux.
Mastering the gaston is essential for tackling complex climbing routes.
The coach demonstrated the proper technique for performing a gaston.
She felt the strain in her arm after holding a gaston for several seconds.

senses.definition: a climbing technique where the climber uses their heel on a hold to pull their body upward or secure a position

examples.x: She executed a perfect heel hook to reach the next hold on the overhang.
He struggled with the move until he employed a heel hook to stabilize himself.
Using a heel hook allowed her to rest momentarily and chalk up before continuing.
The climber relied on a heel hook to transition onto the vertical face.
He felt a surge of confidence after successfully sticking the heel hook.

senses.definition: a dynamic move where the climber jumps or leaps to grab a distant hold

examples.x: He executed a perfect dyno to reach the high handhold on the overhang.
The challenging route required several dynos to complete.
She practiced dynos to improve her explosive power and reach.
Missing the dyno meant falling back to the starting point of the climb.
His favorite climbing gym has a wall dedicated to practicing dynos.
